phase_name: Post-v2.0 ad hoc bug fixes (no active GSD phase)

completed_tasks.name: Eliminate dev-mode StrictMode race in both sign-in forms
Stop showing step 5 in its own per-step deadline list
Sign-out fully clears sessionStorage via clear()
Fix dual-role (materials_readiness) notification under-delivery when assignee gate is set
Remove Lead Site Manager position (data fix, no commit needed)
Reassign Test 2 Project's Assign Designer/Architect for Brief to Tunde Bello (data fix)
Delete QA test accounts (ops.verify@, qa.ops@, verify.admin@trtarredo.demo)
checklists.createdBy nullable + ON DELETE SET NULL (survives submitter deletion)

remaining_tasks.name: Add 'Disputes' link to sidebar with unread badge; badge clears when supervisor attends
Disputes must be actionable for the supervisor — surface as 'action required' (forcing modal / pending-work style), not just a passive link/notification

Larger escalation feature (waive+note by supervisor, Escalations tracking page under Approvals) — user has NOT yet confirmed the proposed design (reuse bypass-approval system vs. build parallel mechanism)
Investigate readiness_forms.project_id schema drift before ever running drizzle-kit push again

blockers.description: drizzle-kit push surfaces an UNRELATED pending change that would DELETE readiness_forms.project_id (6 real rows) if confirmed. Origin of this drift is unknown — not introduced by this session's work.
blockers.workaround: Never run `drizzle-kit push` (interactive or non-interactive/auto-confirm) in this repo until this drift is understood. Apply schema changes via precise, targeted raw SQL (see commits b74f2ba, 23f3f88 for the pattern) instead.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm design for the escalation waive/note/tracking feature (reuse actions/bypass.ts's approval mechanism, scoped to the escalation-target position, vs. a new parallel mechanism)
human_actions_pending.context: Determines the whole implementation shape; asked once, not yet answered

decisions.decision: checklists.createdBy made nullable + ON DELETE SET NULL instead of removing the column or making it optional-at-insert
decisions.rationale: User wants checklist submission data (answers/photos) to survive a submitting user's deletion/resignation without being tied to that user forever; all consuming code already typed/rendered submittedBy as nullable

decisions.decision: checklistDefinitions (templates) needed no change — already has no created_by column at all
decisions.rationale: Verified via schema read; already fully user-independent

next_action: Design + implement the Disputes sidebar entry: unread badge sourced from notifications with type IN ('escalation','bypass_request','pause_flag') AND read_at IS NULL; badge clears when the supervisor opens that project's /disputes/[projectId] thread (mark those notifications read on load). Per the user's latest clarification (2026-07-22), a dispute must ALSO surface as genuine 'action required' for the supervisor — likely meaning it should appear in the same pending/forcing-work surface (lib/my-work.ts's getMyWork `pending` list, and the header 'your turn' pill / forcing modal), not just a bell notification + list page. Confirm this interpretation with the user before building, since it changes lib/my-work.ts's shape (currently only workflow steps populate `pending`, not disputes).
context_notes: Long, very context-heavy session (mostly direct execution, not GSD subagents) doing live bug triage against a real running app + live Neon DB, driven entirely by the user's screenshots/reports rather than a formal roadmap phase. Every fix in this session was independently verified against real/live data (not just unit tests) before committing — that pattern should continue. The escalation/Disputes feature is the one substantial piece of NEW feature work still open; everything else handled this session was a bug fix or data fix. gsd-sdk / phases.clear was NOT used since there's no active phase — this is intentionally an ad hoc quick-task-style session.
